sections.title: Attempting to use any of the facilities before the quest
Pottery wheel, spinning wheel, anvils, furnace, and stile
Attempting to steal from the fish and fur stalls

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: This place? Why, this is Rellekka! Homeland of all Fremennik! I do not recognise your face outerlander; Where do you come from?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's not important...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm... I will not press the issue then outerlander. How may my tribe and I help you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Quests, you say outerlander? Well, I would not call it a quest as such, but if you are brave of heart and strong of body, perhaps...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No, you would not be interested. Forget I said anything, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I would be very interested to hear what you have to offer.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You would? These are unusual sentiments to hear from an outerlander! My suggestion was going to be that if you crave adventure and battle,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: and your heart sings for glory, then perhaps you would be interested in joining our clan, and becoming a Fremennik yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would that involve exactly?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there are two ways to become a member of our clan and call yourself a Fremennik: be born a Fremennik, or be voted in by our council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I've missed the first way, but how can I get the council of elders to vote to let me join your clan?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, that I cannot answer myself. You will need to speak to each of them and see what they require of you as proof of your dedication.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There are twelve council members around this village; you will need to gain a majority vote of at least seven councillors in your favour.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So what say you? Give me the word, and I will tell all of my tribe of your intentions, be they yea or nay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I would enjoy the challenge of becoming an honorary Fremennik. Where and how do I start?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As I say outerlander, you must find and speak to the twelve members of the council of elders, and see what tasks they might set you.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you can gain the support of seven of the twelve, then you will be accepted as one of us without question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way do I want to become like YOU! Forget you, and forget your whole stupid town! You're a bunch of stupid primitives, from what I've seen!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I am sorry to say that I expected such a response from an outerlander. I thought perhaps you might have been different to all the others...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You're right. I really couldn't care less.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: It is as I thought outerlander. Your kind care nothing for the Fremennik, and we feel the same about you. This is the way of the world.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why will no-one talk to me?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Do not take it personally, outerlander! We are a simple people, and it is our experience that keeping ourselves to ourselves is best. This is why speaking to outerlanders is forbidden.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: We do not wish to enter war with the outerlanders and their strange magics, so we limit all unauthorised communication.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then how come you're talking to me?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Ah, this is because I am the chieftain. I am the one who authorises contact. You will not find many of my tribe so forthcoming with you, as I.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there a way for you to authorise your tribe to talk to me then?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there is one way... but I doubt it is of any interest to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I would be very interested to hear what you have to offer.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: You would? These are unusual sentiments to hear from an outerlander! My suggestion was going to be that if you crave adventure and battle,

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: and your heart sings for glory, then perhaps you would be interested in joining our clan, and becoming a Fremennik yourself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What would that involve exactly?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, there are two ways to become a member of our clan and call yourself a Fremennik: be born a Fremennik, or be voted in by our council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I think I've missed the first way, but how can I get the council of elders to vote to let me join your clan?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Well, that I cannot answer myself. You will need to speak to each of them and see what they require of you as proof of your dedication.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There are twelve council members around this village; you will need to gain a majority vote of at least seven councillors in your favour.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: So what say you? Give me the word, and I will tell all of my tribe of your intentions, be they yea or nay.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I think I would enjoy the challenge of becoming an honorary Fremennik. Where and how do I start?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: As I say outerlander, you must find and speak to the twelve members of the council of elders, and see what tasks they might set you.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you can gain the support of seven of the twelve, then you will be accepted as one of us without question.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way do I want to become like YOU! Forget you, and forget your whole stupid town! You're a bunch of stupid primitives, from what I've seen!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I am sorry to say that I expected such a response from an outerlander. I thought perhaps you might have been different to all the others...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any quests?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Quests, you say outerlander? Well, I would not call it a quest as such, but if you are brave of heart and strong of body, perhaps...

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: No, you would not be interested. Forget I said anything, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know this is a bit of a long shot... but are you a member of the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Bjorn
lines.text: I'm now a member of the counshil of eblars? Thanksh a lot buddy! (hic) Letsh have a drink to celebrate!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... I'll take that as a no.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I know this is a bit of a long shot... but are you a member of the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Eldgrim
lines.text: Counshil alwaysh meshing up my drinksh 'do thish, do that, pass this tesht something something something something'.. man I hate that counshil.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're not then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for a member of the council of elders who will support my application to become an honorary Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Inga
lines.text: I'm a council member.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I somehow persuade you to vote for me at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Inga
lines.text: No. Frankly, I don't like you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you don't even know me!

lines.speaker: Inga
lines.text: You also smell bad. And have stupid hair. And bad breath. And you walk a bit funny.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I didn't come all the way up here just to be insulted!

lines.speaker: Inga
lines.text: No, I'm quite sure you could have found plenty of people to insult you wherever your home is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what? Forget you, you stupid barbarian! I don't need your stupid vote!

lines.speaker: Inga
lines.text: Tch, typical outerlander rudeness. I'm glad I didn't vote for you now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Freidir
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi. Are you a member of the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Me? On the council? Ha ha ha ha! Sorry buddy, I'm not a 'real' Fremennik yet! Let me guess, you're trying to get some council members to vote for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right, to become a Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: You and me both buddy! We're both doing the same trials! This is my trial of manhood! How you finding it so far? Pretty easy, huh buddy?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's okay I guess...

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Yeah, that's the spirit buddy! It's all real easy, I wouldn't worry yourself about it, you'll be quaffing beers in the longhall as a Fremennik in no time!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Lensa
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Jennella
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you know any council members?

lines.speaker: Sassilik
lines.text: I know that I am one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Can you vote for me at the council of elders for me to become a Fremennik?

lines.speaker: Sassilik
lines.text: Sure. On one condition.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: (sigh) What condition?

lines.speaker: Sassilik
lines.text: Actually there is no condition! I don't want to vote for you! You seem too gullible to be a Fremennik!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's kind of mean to trick me like that...

lines.speaker: Sassilik
lines.text: What do I care? I am a Fremennik and you are just some stupid outerlander! You stupid outerlander!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the Fremmenik council?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Indeed I am, outerlander. Why do you ask?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I was told by Brundt that I could be voted into the tribe as an honorary Fremmenik if I could find seven members of the council of elders to vote in my favour.

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Aye, that is indeed true.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I have your vote?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: You can have my vote on the day the skies turn red, the waters turn pink, the rocks turn yellow and the sun turns black.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Um... do you have any estimate on when that is?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: I'm telling you I will never vote for you, you stupid outlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: No. Weapons are my speciality. Not politics.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, thanks.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Freygerd
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Reeso
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Borrokar
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello, are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Lanzig
lines.text: I am afraid not outerlander. Goodbye.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi, I don't suppose you are a member of the council of elders are you?

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: No, outerlander, I have never had the honour to be asked.

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: I am but a lowly warrior, fighting for my people wherever threats may appear against us...

lines.speaker: Jarvald
lines.text: So what do you want from me, outerlander?

sections.title: Talking to Manni the Reveller again
Talking to Manni after the player's loss

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do I have to do to earn your vote again?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Beat me in a drinking contest! Grab a keg of beer from that table near the bar, and come back here with it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh yeah. Okay, I will do.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Ah, I see you have your keg of beer. Are we ready to drink against each other?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: As, I see you have your keg of beer. Are we ready to drink against each other?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's start this drinking contest!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: As you wish, outerlander; I will drink first, then you will drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ish no' fair! (hic) I canna drink another drop! Alsho feel veddy, veddy ill...

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I guessh I win then ouddaladder! (hic) Niche try, anyway!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Come back if'n you fanshy a rematch! (hic) Jusht let me have a coffee firsht...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm not quite ready yet...

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Want to line your stomach with some food or something, huh outerlander? Come back to me when you're ready to drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Hello outerlander. I overheard your conversation with Brundt just now. You wish to become a member of the Fremennik?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's right! Why, are you on the council?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Do not let my drink-soused appearance fool you, I earnt my place on the council many years past. I am always glad to see new blood enter our tribe, and will happily vote for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: ...Providing you can pass a little test for me. As a Fremennik, you will need to show cunning, stamina, fortitude, and an iron constitution. I know of only one way to test all of these.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what's that?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Why, a drinking contest!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: The task is simple enough! You versus me, a stiff drink each, last man standing wins the trial. So what say you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A drinking contest? Easy. Set them up, and I'll knock them back.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: When you are ready to begin, go and pick up a keg from that table over there, and come back here.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: We start when you have your keg of beer with you, and finish when one of us can drink no more and yields.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think drinking myself into a stupor is a very good way of testing my worthiness.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Well, perhaps it's not, but it's my way. If you want my vote at the council, I'm afraid you're going to have to drink me under the table.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to find someone else to vote for me then.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: As you wish, outerlander.

sections.title: Using a normal beer or beer tankard on the Council workman

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Ta very much, like. That'll hit the spot nicely. Here, you can have this. I picked it up as a souvenir on me last hols.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: I dunno rightly, but if you use a tinderbox on it, it don't half make a loud noise!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: How do. You planning on crossing this here bridge and heading up to Rellekka then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, actually I was.

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Aye, 'tis a good thing we fixed this 'ere bridge, I reckon. You best be careful up there. Them Fremenniks are odd 'uns.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you fixed this bridge yourself?

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Aye, that I did. 'Twas real thirsty work too. If only some kind stranger would buy us a bit of beer to sup, eh?

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: What with that inn at seers so close by and all, eh?

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Just make sure yer don't get me none of that non alcoholic rubbish from that poison salesman guy at the tavern!

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Aye, that's what I told t'gaffer. Nobody wants to go up and hang out with a buncha dumb barbarians when there's a perfectly good inn to sup in, in t'seers village.

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Ain't much better in life than having a nice cold beer to sup on a hot day like this, eh? Better'n fixing bridges, eh?

lines.speaker: Council workman
lines.text: Just make sure yer don't get me none of that non alcoholic rubbish from that poison salesman guy at the tavern!

sections.title: Talking to the Poison Salesman again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I understand you have some low alcohol beer for sale...?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: That I do, and sales are rocketing upwards as word of mouth spreads of it's clean taste and cool flavour! So... you want to buy some?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Howdy! You seem like someone with discerning taste! Howsabout you try my brand new range of alcohol?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Didn't you used to sell poison?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: That I did, indeed! Peter Potter's Patented Multipurpose poison! A miracle of modern apothecarys! My exclusive concoction has been tested on...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh, yeah, I've already heard the sales pitch.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Sorry stranger, old habits die hard I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you don't sell poison any more?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Well, I would, but I ran out of stock. Business wasn't helped with that stuff that happened up at the Sinclair Mansion much either, I'll be honest.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: So, being the man of enterprise that I am I decided to branch out a little bit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Into alcohol?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Absolutely! The basic premise between alcohol and poison is pretty much the same, after all! The difference is that my alcohol has a unique property others do not!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is that?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Ever been too drunk to find your own home? Ever wished that you could party away all night long, and still wake up fresh as a daisy the next morning?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Thanks to the miracles of modern magic we have come up with just the solution you need! Peter Potter's Patented Party Potions!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: It looks just like beer! It tastes just like beer! It smells just like beer! But... it's not beer!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Our mages have mused for many moments to bring you this miracle of modern magic! It has all the great tastes you'd expect, but contains absolutely no alcohol!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: That's right! You can drink Peter Potters Patented Party Potion as much as you want, and suffer absolutely no ill effects whatsoever!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: The clean fresh taste you know you can trust, from the people who brought you; Peter Potters Patented multipurpose poison, Peter Potters peculiar paint packs

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: and Peter Potters paralysing panic pins. Available now from all good stockists! Ask your local bartender now, and experience the taste revolution of the century!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So... when you say 'all good stockists'...

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Yes?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How many inns actually sell this stuff?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Well... nobody has actually bought any yet. Everyone I try and sell it to always asks me what exactly the point of beer that has absolutely no effect on you is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what is the point?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Well... Um... Er... Hmmm. You, er, don't get drunk.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Aw man... You don't want any now do you? I've really tried to push this product, but I just don't think the world is ready for beer that doesn't get you drunk.

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: I'm a man ahead of my time I tell you! It's not that my products are bad, it's that they're too good for the market!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I would like some. How much do you want for it?

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Y-you would??? Um, okay! I knew I still had the old salesmans skills going on!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: I'll sell you a keg of it for only 250 gold pieces! So what do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Sorry pal, we do not offer credit for any purchases made of Peter Potter's patented party potion! Come back when you have the cash!

lines.speaker: Poison Salesman
lines.text: Sorry pal, doesn't look like you have room on you to carry it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, not really.

sections.title: Attempting to use the low alcohol keg with the regular keg of beer

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Thinking of cheating my test, outerlander? I don't think so! You drink that keg fairly, or not at all! Don't let me catch you cheating!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm going to have to find some way to distract this guy while I cheat...

sections.title: Talking to Manni after switching with the low alcohol beer

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Ah, I see you have your keg of beer. Are we ready to drink against each other?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, let's start this drinking contest!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: As you wish outerlander; I will drink first, then you will drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Aaaah, lovely stuff. So you want to get the next round in, or shall I? You don't look so good there!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Wassha? Guh? You drank that whole keg! But it dinna affect you at all! I conshede! You can probably outdrink me!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I jusht can't (hic) believe it! Thatsh shome mighty fine drinking legs you got! Anyone who can drink like THAT getsh my vote atta consh.. counsh... gets my vote!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ow!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't have anything I really want to put inside a smelly old drain pipe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That is going to make a really loud bang when it goes off! It would be a perfect distraction to help me cheat in the drinking contest!

lines.speaker: Everyone in the longhall
lines.text: what was THAT?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can rely on your vote at the council?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Absholutely! (hic) You're one mighty drinker!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how would I go about writing this epic?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Well, first of all you are going to need an instrument. Like all true bards you are going to have to make this yourself.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do I make an instrument?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Well, it is a long and drawn-out process. Just east of this village there is an unusually musical tree that can be used to make very high quality instruments.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Cut a piece from it, and then carve it into a special shape that will allow you to string it. Using a knife as you would craft any other wooden object would be best for this.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then what do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Next you will need to string your lyre. There is a troll to the South-east who has some golden wool. I would not recommend using anything else to string your lyre with.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Anything else?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Well, when you have crafted your lyre you will need the blessing of the Fossegrimen to tune your lyre to perfection before you even consider a public performance.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who or what is Fossegrimen?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Fossegrimen is a lake spirit that lives just a little way South-west of this village. Make her an offering of fish, and you will then be ready for your performance.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Make sure you give her a suitable offering however. If the offering is found to be unworthy, then you may find yourself unable to play your lyre with any skill at all!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what would be a worthy offering?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: A raw shark, manta ray, or sea turtle should be sufficient as an offering.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, what else do I need to do?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: When you have crafted your lyre and been blessed by Fossegrimen, then you will finally be ready to make your performance to the revellers at the longhall.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Head past the bouncers and onto the stage, and then begin to play. If all goes well, you should find the music spring to your mind and sing your own epic on the spot.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: I will observe both you and the audience, and if you show enough talent, I will happily vote in your favour at the council of elders.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Is that clear enough, outerlander? Would you like me to repeat anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me about making a lyre again, please Olaf?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: There are three parts to making the lyre; First you need to cut some special wood from the special musical tree which can be found just east of Rellekka.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: When you have the wood, you will need to use a knife to craft it into a Lyre. When you have an unstrung lyre, you will need to string it before you can play it.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: I would not use anything other than the golden string of Lalli, a troll who lives in a cave just to the South-east.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Is that clear enough, outerlander? Would you like me to repeat anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me about getting a blessing from Fossegrimen again?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Certainly outerlander! Fossegrimen is a lake spirit who lives in the lake just to the South-west of here. She will bless you so that you may play your lyre,

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: if you give her a worthy offering. She will only accept raw fish, so place a raw fish on her pedestal, and she will bless you. The better the offering, the longer

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: the amount of time you will be blessed for. You will not be able to perform on stage without the blessing of the Fossegrimen.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Is that clear enough, outerlander? Would you like me to repeat anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you remind me about how to perform at the longhall again please?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Not a problem outerlander. When you have your lyre and have been blessed, you will be able to pass the bouncer at the stage entrance of the longhall.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: He will not let you pass unless he believes you are ready to perform on stage. Simply go past him and onto the stage and use your lyre, and you will

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: begin to play to entertain the revellers. If all has gone well, you will feel the music come to your mind, unbidden, as you sing an epic of your exploits.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: I will watch your performance from backstage, and also study how much the crowd likes you, and if I believe you have enough talent, then I will vote for you at the council.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Is that clear enough, outerlander? Would you like me to repeat anything?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I think that's all pretty straight forward. I'll go and make my lyre now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Bah! Puny humans always try steal Lallis' golden apples! You go away now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, I'm not after your golden apples. I was wondering if I could have some golden wool; I need it to string a lyre.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Ha! You not fool me human! Me am smart! Other trolls so jealous of how brainy I are, they kick me out of camp and make me live down here in cave! But me have last funny!

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Me find golden apples on tree and me build wall to stop anyone who not Lalli eating lovely golden apples! Did me not tell you I are smart?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, yes, you are incredibly clever. Now please can I have some golden wool?

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Hum, me think you not really think I are clever. Me think you is trying trick Lalli. Me not like you as much as other human. He give me present. I give him wool.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Other human? You mean someone else has been there and you gave them wool?

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Human call itself Askeladden! It not trick Lalli, Lalli do good deal with human! Stupid human get some dumb wool, but did not get golden apples!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I see... okay, well, bye!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, honest, you're REALLY clever. Why, I could not contemplate a mere chicanery with an advanced intellect such as you currently present.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Grrr! Why you insult me stupid human? Me know it are because you jealous of Lalli's huge brain and you try steal apples!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I give you a present? Or maybe exchange you something for some wool?

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Ha! You stupid human! You think you trick Lalli? Me know you want my golden apples Well! You not get them! HAHAHA!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... okay then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So this is the exact same type of rock you managed to sell to Lalli as a pet?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Yup, sure is buddy! That Lalli is stupid, even by troll standards!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have another rock? I lost mine...

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Sure thing buddy! I'd say take better care of this one, but it's just a rock! I have hundreds of them! Go wild!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there. I understand you managed to get some golden wool from Lalli?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: HAHAHA! Yeah, that Lalli... what a maroon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how did you manage to get the wool?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Well, as you know, I am doing the same trials that you are as part of my test of manhood, and that troll is the only one who can get that wool.

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: You might have noticed he's kind of... messed in the head buddy! He's real paranoid about people stealing his golden apples, isn't he?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed he is. So how did you manage to get some golden wool from him?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: It was easy buddy! I persuaded him he needed a pet to help him guard his apples. A pet that would never sleep! A pet that would never need food, or exercise!

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: A pet that would never need him to clean up its... well, you know, buddy. A pet that would always be loyal to him! A faithful companion for life!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What pet is this then?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: A pet ROCK!

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Man, can you believe that stupid troll traded me some of his golden wool for a worthless ROCK?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Buddy, I hafta say; if brains were explosives, that troll wouldn't have enough to blow his nose!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you have any spare rocks then?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Sure thing buddy, although I have to say, I doubt even that troll is stupid enough to fall for the SAME trick TWICE in a row! You can try anyway though!

sections.title: Talking to Lalli again with the pet rock

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Bah! Puny humans always try steal Lallis' golden apples! You go away now!

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Unless you got tasty food for Lalli. Then I might trade you for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually I don't...

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Hah! Me knew it! You human just try trick Lalli into giving away golden apple! Me not as stupid as you look!

sections.title: Talking to Lalli after making the soup

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Your soup very tasty, human! But me still not want trade golden apples for your stone. Me think pet rock get jealous.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I. DON'T. WANT. ANY. GOLDEN. APPLES. ALL. I. WANT. IS. A. GOLDEN. FLEECE.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Gee, sorry human, all you have do is ask, me not need you to shout. You act like you think Lalli am stupid or something...

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Here you go. Hah! Me trick you human! All you got is worthless golden fleece! Me got very rare soup-making stone!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Glad you're happy Lalli!

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: It am ready now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not just yet...

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: It am ready now?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed it is. Try it and see.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Hmm... YUM! That are delicious! Me never know human know to make soup out of stone? It some special stone?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Indeed it is. But I'm willing to trade it.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Let me think about that, me like to think.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I really wouldn't know where to begin playing anything on this...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there.

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Bah! Puny humans always try steal Lallis' golden apples! You go away now!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Wait! I have something that might interest you... a pet rock!

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Hah! You think me stupid or something human? Me already got one! Me don't want lotta baby rocks either, so me don't want another one around the place!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Please... all I want is some of your golden wool...

lines.speaker: Lalli
lines.text: Stupid human think he can trick me into giving away some golden apples! Hah! Golden apples is all me got to eat, you not get them, no way!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm... So you're hungry? I think I will have the perfect thing for you to eat... I just need to get myself an onion, a potato and a cabbage...

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: Many thanks for the offering outerlander. Please accept as a gift the ability to play your lyre...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Dor Ray Me So.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fah La Ti Doh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: La La La La.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: De-Doo-De-Doo.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: What can I do for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hear that I can present you with an offering and you will enchant my lyre for me.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: This is true. For a simple enchantment, you may place an offering on my Altar. Can I help you with anything else?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nothing, sorry.

lines.speaker: Fossegrimen
lines.text: Do not summon me for idle conversation! Come back when you have an offering.

sections.title: Talking to the Longhall with the blessed lyre

lines.speaker: Longhall Bouncer
lines.text: You have an instrument now, huh? Go on through the door.

lines.speaker: Longhall Bouncer
lines.text: Yeah, you're good to go through. Olaf tells me you're some kind of outerlander bard here on tour. I doubt you're worse than Olaf is.

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Hey, I've done that quest too!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: I have met cows more musical than you!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: I thought somebody was murdering a cat!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Is your singing some kind of outerlander weapon?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: My grandmother could do better, and she is dead!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Please, ye gods! Make it stop!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: would torture us so, outerlander?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Worst. Bard. EVER.

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: You call THAT singing?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Don't give up your day job outerlander!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: I do such feats before breakfast!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Never before have mine ears been so offended!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Your song makes no sense outerlander!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: You know, I actually quite liked that verse...

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: That's kind of catchy. Irritating. But catchy.

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Please... Kill me now...

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: looting and drinking part?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: When do you get to the good parts?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Truly, this is the worst sound I have ever heard!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: What kind of bard are you anyway?

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: Please tell me that was the last verse...

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: The screams of my dying comrades sound better!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: I have produced better sounds in the outhouse!

lines.speaker: [Fridgeir/Ospak/Styrmir/Torbrund]
lines.text: YOU ARE A TERRIBLE BARD!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The thought of lots of questing

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: leaves some people unfulfilled,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: but I have done my simple best

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: in entering the Champions' Guild.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When it comes to fighting

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I hit my share of zeroes

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But I'm well respected at

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: the Guild reserved for Heroes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I cannot even start to list

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: the amount of foes I've killed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I will simply tell you this:

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've joined the Legends' Guild!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: When people speak of training

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: some people think they're fine.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But they just all seem jealous that

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: my [skill]'s ninety-nine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: [Player name] is my name,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I haven't much to say.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But since I have to sing this song,

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll just go ahead and play.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Wow! That was awesome! You are one of the greatest bards I have ever had the pleasure of watching performing! You have certainly earned my vote! I hope we can duet together soon!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I rely on your vote with the council of elders in my favour?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: You have a truly poetic soul! Anyone who can compose such a beautiful epic, and then perform it so flawlessly can only bring good to our clan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... so that's a yes, then?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Absolutely! We must collaborate together on a duet sometime, don't you think?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Hello? Yes? You want something, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Why, indeed I am, outerlander! My talents as an exemplary musician made it difficult for them not to accept me! Why do you wish to know this?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I ask because I am currently doing the Fremennik trials so as to join your clan. I need seven of the twelve council of elders to vote for me.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Ahhh... and you wish to earn my vote? I will gladly accept you as a Fremennik should you be able to prove yourself to have a little musical ability!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how would I do that?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Why, by playing in our longhall! All you need to do is impress the revellers there with a few verses of an epic of your own creation!

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: So what say you outerlander? Are you up for the challenge?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sure! This certainly sounds pretty easy to accomplish - I'll have your vote in no time!

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: That is great news outerlander! We always need more music lovers here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it! This all sounds like way too much work for a stupid vote at a meeting! I'll find someone else instead.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Hello again outerlander. Have you managed to hunt down the terrible Draugen yet?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet...

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Hmmm. Laziness is not a trait that I admire. I suggest you go and do so if you wish me to vouchsafe for you to our council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not yet... I lost the talisman you gave me.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: The typical careless regard for valuable objects I would expect from an outerlander such as yourself. Do not lose THIS one.

sections.title: Talking to Sigli after defeating The Draugen

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Absolutely outerlander! Anyone brave enough to hunt and defeat the Draugen will be a great addition to our clan!

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: I saw the entire hunt. Let me take that talisman from you; I would be honoured to speak out for you to our council of elders after such a hunt, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: What do you want outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: The Fremennik council of elders? I am pleased to say that I am. My value as a huntsman is recognised by my position there.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if I could persuade you to vouch for me as a member of your clan?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: You? ... well... I am not totally against the idea outerlander. If you can demonstrate some hunting skills then perhaps I may offer my vote.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I prove my hunting skills to you? I can go kill, like, a hundred chickens for you right now!

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Chickens? You think that would impress me?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cows then? I can kill cows until, er, the cows come home.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: No. The prey I have in mind for you to prove your worth to me is something far more dangerous. Far more difficult. Far more deadly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not... Giant Rats?!?!

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: I suspect you are mocking me outerlander. You will need to prove your skill as a hunter to me by tracking and defeating... The Draugen.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's a Draugen? Some kind of cheap barbarian rip- off of a dragon?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Hmmm. No, the words are slightly similar I suppose, but they are very different creatures.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: The Draugen is an evil ghost from Fremennik mythology, that devours the souls of those brave warriors who meet their ends at sea.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: It stalks the coastlines, invisible to all. It brings us bad fortunes, and curses our journeys across the seas. It is also unkillable by normal means.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...Let me get this straight; You want me to hunt an unkillable, invincible, and invisible enemy? How am I supposed to do that?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Well outerlander, should you accept my challenge I will show you a special hunter's trick that will help you. Do you accept the challenge?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, I need every vote I can get in the council of elders, but this certainly sounds impossible to do...

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Not at all outerlander. The Draugen is indeed impossible to kill, but that is not the same as being impossible to fight against.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Every time he takes a Fremennik life, he gains in power, so to keep it from becoming too powerful we hunters hunt it and steal its life force.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: To defeat the Draugen you will need a special item; I have one to give you but it seems as though your inventory is too full to accept it.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: We do this with a special talisman. Here, take it; it will let you track the Draugen while it's invisible, and when you defeat it will absorb its essence.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: I want you to track the Draugen, defeat it, and store its essence in that talisman for me. If you can do this important task for my clan, I will vote for you.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Take care of the talisman, and see me when you have completed this task.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Forget it! This all sounds like way too much work for a stupid vote at a meeting! I'll find someone else instead.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: So... how goes it outerlander? Did you manage to obtain my flower for me yet? Or do you lack the necessary merchanting skills?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm still working on it... Do you have any suggestion where to start looking for it?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: I suggest you ask around the other Fremennik in the town. A good merchant will find exactly what their customer needs somewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Nope, sorry buddy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I wouldn't know about that, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings again outerlander! How goes your attempts to gain votes with the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Not really my affair, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: I'm sorry, no I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Not in the slightest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: I never saw anything like that in my store before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: I never saw anything like that in my store before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I'm afraid not, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: No... sorry, I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I have not the first idea about that.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Greetings outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: No idea at all outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a rare flower from across the sea, do you?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Ah! Even the outerlanders have heard of my mysterious flower! I found it in a country far far away from here!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I buy it from you?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: I'm afraid not, outerlander. There is a woman in this village whose heart I seek to capture, and I think giving her this strange flower might be my best bet with her.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Maybe you could let me have the flower and do something else to impress her?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Hmm... that is not a totally stupid idea outerlander. I know she is a lover of music, and a romantic ballad might be just the thing with which to woo her.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Unfortunately I don't have a musical bone in my entire body, so someone else will have to write it for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So if I can find someone to write you a romantic ballad, you will give me your flower?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: That sounds like a fair deal to me, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a love ballad, do you?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Well, as official Fremennik bard, it falls within my remit to compose all music for the tribe. I am fully versed in all the various types of romantic music.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Can you write me one then?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Well... normally I would be thrilled at the chance to show my skill as a poet in composing a seductively romantic ballad...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: let me guess; here comes the 'but'.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: ...but unfortunately I cannot concentrate fully upon my work recently.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Why is that then?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: It is these old worn out shoes of mine... As a bard I am expected to wander the lands, singing of the glorious battles of our warriors.

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: If you can find me a pair of sturdy boots to replace these old worn out ones of mine, I will be happy to spend the time on composing you a romantic ballad.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a guarantee of a reduction on sales taxes, do you?

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Yes I do outerlander. Only the Chieftain may permit such a thing. Talk to him.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find some custom sturdy boots, do you?

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Well, I don't usually have many shoes in stock here in my little clothes shop... I will be able to make you up a pair if you are really desperate though?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: They're not for me... I need them for Olaf.

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Oh, that foolish bard... Why didn't he just ask me to make him some? It is his stupid pride, I believe!

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: I will tell you what I will do outerlander; I know that you must have the ear of the chieftain for him to consider you as worthy of becoming a Fremennik by trial.

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: I will make you a pair of sturdy boots for Olaf if you will persuade him to reduce the sales tax placed upon all Fremennik shopkeepers. It does nothing but hurt my business now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I will see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings again outerlander! How goes your attempts to gain votes with the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a guarantee of a reduction on sales taxes, do you?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: A reduction on sales taxes? Why, I am the only one in the Fremennik who may authorise such a thing. What does an outerlander want with that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Actually, it's not for me. I need to get it as part of my trials.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm. Interesting. Your trials seem to be very different to those I took as a young lad. Well, I am not adverse in principle to giving a slight tax break to our shops.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: There will of course be a shortfall in the tribe's income, that will need to be made up for elsewhere, however.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: How about this. For many years Sigli has been the only one in the tribe who knows the locations of the best hunting grounds where game is easiest to catch.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you can persuade him to let the entire tribe know these hunting grounds, then we can increase productivity within the tribe, and any shortfall caused

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: by lowering sales taxes will be covered. I think this is a more than fair arrangement to make, don't you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, that sounds very fair.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Speak to Sigli then, and you may have my promise to reduce our sales taxes. And best of luck with the rest of your trials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Nope, sorry buddy!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: I'm sorry, no I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Not in the slightest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: I never saw anything like that in my store before.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Not really my affair, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: No... sorry, I don't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I'm afraid not, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I have not the first idea about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a finely balanced custom bowstring, do you?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: Aye, I have a few in stock. What would an outerlander be wanting with equipment like that?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's for Sigli. It needs to be weighted precisely to suit his hunting bow.

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: For Sigli eh? Well, I made his bow in the first place, so I'll be able to select the right string for you... just one small problem.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's that?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: This string you'll be wanting... Very rare. Take a lot of time to recreate. Not sure you have the cash for it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then maybe you'll accept something else...?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: Heh. Good thinking outerlander. Well, it's true, there is more to life than just making money. Making weapons is good money, but it's not why I do it.

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: I'll tell you what. I heard a rumour that one of the fishermen down by the docks caught some weird looking fish as they were fishing the other day.

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: From what I hear this fish is unique. Nobody's ever seen its like before. This intrigues me. I'd like to have it for myself. Make a good trophy.

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: You get me that fish, I give you the bowstring. What do you say? We got a deal?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find an exotic and extremely rare fish, do you?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: What? There's another one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Er... no, it's the one for you that I'm looking for...

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: Ah. I see. I already told you. Some guy down by the docks was bragging. Best ask there, I reckon.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Greetings outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a map to unspoiled hunting grounds, do you?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Well, of course I do. I wouldn't be much of a huntsman if I didn't know where to find my prey now, would I outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, I guess not. So can I have it?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Directions to my hunting grounds could mean the end of my livelihood. The only way I would be prepared to give them up would be...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What? Power? Money? Women? Wine?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: ...a new string for my hunting bow. Not just any bowstring; I need a custom bowstring, balanced for my bow precisely to keep my hunt competitive.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Only in this way would I allow the knowledge of my hunting grounds to be passed on to strangers.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So where would I get that?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: I have no idea. But then again, I'm happy with my old bowstring and being the only person who knows where my hunting ground is.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a map of deep sea fishing spots do you?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: You should pay attention when I speak! I already told you, that rip off navigator has it, and I want it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find an exotic and extremely rare fish, do you?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Ah, so even outerlanders have heard of my amazing catch the other day!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You have it? Can I trade you something for it?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: As exotic looking as it is, it is bad eating. I will happily trade it if you can find me the secret map of the best fishing spots that the navigator has hidden away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that all?

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Indeed it is, outerlander. The only reason I sit out here in the cold all day long is so I don't have to pay his outrageous prices.

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: By getting me his copy of that map, I will finally be self sufficient. I might even make a profit!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Greetings outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a weather forecast from the Fremennik Seer do you?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Uh... from the Seer perhaps?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a weather forecast from the Fremennik Seer do you?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Er.... Yes, because I AM the Fremennik Seer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a weather forecast then please?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You require a divination of the weather? This is a simple matter for me, but I will require something in return from you for this small service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I knew you were going to say that...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Do not fret, outerlander; it is a fairly simple matter. I require a bodyguard for protection. Find someone willing to offer me this service.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is all.

sections.title: Talking to Peer the Seer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a brave and powerful warrior to act as a bodyguard?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: If I did, then I would simply have asked them myself now, wouldn't I, outerlander?

sections.title: Convincing Thorvald to be a bodyguard

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a brave and powerful warrior to act as a bodyguard?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Know you not who I am outerlander? There are none more brave or powerful than me amongst all the Fremennik!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: However... The role of bodyguard is below me, as a noble warrior. You might as well ask me to babysit the children!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is there no way you would do this for me?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: There is but one way outerlander. Since I was steeled in battle, I have dreamt of earning my place at the Champions Table in the Long Hall.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: It is a tradition amongst us that the bravest and strongest are honoured with a table of champions to drink and feast all that they can in our Long Hall.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Unfortunately, there are only a fixed number of places available at the table, and these places were all filled many moons ago by others.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Although my worthiness is undeniable, the only way I may take my place is if one of those already there die, or give up their place to me voluntarily.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you want me to go kill one of them off for you? Make it look like an accident?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: WHAT? No, no, not at all! I am shocked you would suggest such a thing!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: If you can persuade one of the Revellers to give up their Champions' Token to you so that I might take their place, you may have my contract as a bodyguard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay, I'll see what I can do.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a Champions' Token?

lines.speaker: Thorvald
lines.text: If you can persuade one of the Revellers to give up their Champions' Token to you so that I might take their place, you may have my contract as a bodyguard.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a token to allow a seat at the champions table, do you?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: As a matter of fact, I do. I have one right here. I earnt my place here at the longhall for surviving over 5000 battles and raiding parties.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Due to my contribution to the tribe, I am now permitted to spend my days here in the longhall listening to the epic tales of the bard, and drinking beer.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Cool. That sounds pretty sweet! So I guess you don't want to give it away?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I think it sounds better than it actually is outerlander. I miss my glory days of combat on the battlefield. And to tell you the truth, the beer here isn't great, and the bards' music is lousy.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I would happily give up my token if it were not for the one thing that keeps me here. Our barkeep is one of the best in the world, and has worked in taverns across the land.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: When she was younger, she experimented a lot with her drinks, and invented a cocktail so alcoholic and tasty that it has become something of a legend to all who enjoy a drink.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Unfortunately, she decided that cocktails were not a suitable drink for Fremennik warriors, and vowed to never again make it.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: I have been here every day since she returned, hoping that someday she might change her mind and I might try this legendary cocktail for myself. Alas, it has never come to pass...

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: If you can persuade her to make me her legendary cocktail, I will be happy to never let another drop of alcohol pass my lips, and will give you my champions token.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: That's all.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find the longhall barkeeps' legendary cocktail, do you?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Uh... yes, the longhall barkeep has it. So could you get me my drink now please?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find the longhall barkeeps' legendary cocktail, do you?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: How did you hear about that?!?!?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I didn't think anybody knew about that... Well, it is true that in my younger years as a barkeep, I wandered the lands trying various alcoholic delicacies.

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Did you ever realise just how many different types of alcohol there are here in Gielinor? Lots!

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Well, anyway, I used a fusion of various drinks from all around the world to create the greatest cocktail ever made!

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Of course, when my wanderlust was gone, and I returned back to Rellekka to serve as barkeep here, I gave all that up.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But you still remember how to make it, right?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Of course.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And you have all the ingredients here? I don't need to go chasing round the world for obscure ingredients to make it?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: No, I have them all here. Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make me your legendary cocktail then?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I would rather not; it is a reminder of a life I left behind when I came back.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any way I could change your mind?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: You need this to become a Fremennik, right? Well, you seem okay for an outerlander, it would be a shame to see you fail. You know Askeladden?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That kid outside? Sure.

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: He is nothing but a pest. He keeps sneaking in and stealing beer. I shudder to think what he will be like when he has passed his trial of manhood,

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: and is allowed in here legitimately. If you can get him to sign a contract promising that he will NEVER EVER EVER darken my doorway here again, you get the drink.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Any idea how I can get him to do that?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Knowing that little horror, he'll probably be willing to in exchange for some cash. You should go ask him yourself though.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a written promise from Askeladden to stay out of the Longhall?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Well, as I say, you should talk to him about that. Knowing the little runt as I do though He'll probably do it for the cash.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a written promise from Askeladden to stay out of the Longhall?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: What? I can't believe she asked you to get a written promise from me to stay out!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yup, she really did.

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Awwwwwww.... but the longhall is just SO MUCH FUN! I'd live there if I could! I suppose you really need that promise to help become a Fremennik, huh?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I really do...

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Well I'll tell you what buddy; As it's you, I'll give you that written promise. All I ask in return for it is a measly 5000 gold. What do you say?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That's all you want in return? Sure thing. Here you go.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: ...how embarrassing. I appear to be short...

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Trying to scam me, huh buddy? Well it won't work! You come back with the cash, or don't come back at all!

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Done, and done. Let me know if you got any more cash burning a hole in your pocket I can relieve you of, buddy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't think so... That's really quite a lot of money...

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Hey, suit yourself buddy. You change your mind, the bank of Askeladden is open for deposits 24 hours a day! Eh-heh-heh-heh-heh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I thought you really liked the long hall?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: I do!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Then why did you sign this guarantee that you will never enter it again?

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: Aha! It is because I am cunning! That guarantee says that Askeladden will never enter the longhall again! But when I have completed my Fremennik trials,

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: and passed my trial of manhood, I will be given a new name, as is our custom, and will therefore not be Askeladden anymore! That guarantee isn't worth the paper it's written on!!

lines.speaker: Askeladden
lines.text: You didn't think I would give up going to the longhall for only 5000 did you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Knowing you, I guess I didn't.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi! Can I please have one of your legendary cocktails now?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: What?!?! I can't believe you... Let me look at that... Askeladden would NEVER... Gosh. It looks legitimate.

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Here you go, on the house! You have made my life SO much easier! Knowing that little monster won't be bugging me in here all the time anymore!

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: That little weasel will have to abide by this written promise that Askeladden can never ever enter the Longhall again! He can't get round this one!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Uh... yeah... yeah, you probably won't see someone called Askeladden coming in here...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks for making me this cocktail. Why don't you make them anymore normally?

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Ah... when I gave up my travels across the world many years back, to return to my expected role as longhall barkeep, as my mother, and her mother, were before me,

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I gave up a lot of the freedom I had found in the outside world. I know it is our custom to shun outerlanders and their ways, but I didn't find them as bad as the stories say.

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: Sometimes I feel as though we Fremennik live in a prison that we have constructed for ourselves, and that WE are the outerlanders, out here on the edge of the world...

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: I'm sorry, I think it is part of the job of Longhall barkeep to get philosophical about things ocassionally. I wish you all the best of luck with your trials, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Thora the Barkeep
lines.text: When you have finished, perhaps you will come back here, and we can share a drink over tales of the outside world?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks, I'd like that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey. I got your cocktail for you.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: ...It is true! The legendary cocktail! I have waited for this day ever since I first started drinking!

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Here outerlander, you may take my token. I will happily give up my place at the longhalls table of champions just for a taste of this exquisite beverage!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's just a drink...

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: No, it is an artform. A drink such as this should be appreciated, and admired. It is like a fine painting, or a tasteful sculpture.

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: If what I hear is true, then all other drinks become like unpalatable water in comparison to this!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I guess you're happy with the trade then!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it doesn't bother you at all that you just gave up your place here for one drink?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Ah, but it was not just any drink... It was the finest cocktail ever created! Now that I have tasted it, I need never drink again, for my tastebuds will never be so excited!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So it was nice?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: It was... exquisite!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What did it taste of, then?

lines.speaker: Manni the Reveller
lines.text: Mostly tomato juice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like your contract to offer your services as a bodyguard.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Oh you would, would you outerlander? I have already told you, I will not demean myself with such a baby sitting job until I can sit in the Longhall with pride.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: It's a good thing I have the Champions' Token right here then, isn't it?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Ah... well this is a different matter. With that token I can claim my rightful place as a champion in the Long hall!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Here outerlander, I can suffer the indignity of playing babysitter if it means that I can then revel with my warrior equals in the Long Hall afterwards!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Here outerlander, take this contract; I will fulfill it to my utmost.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You didn't take much persuading to 'lower' yourself to a bodyguard.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: You misunderstand, outerlander. Normally I will only battle for a noble cause, but have never been recognised as a true champion here.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: With this Champion's token, I can stand alongside my warrior brethren in the Long Hall, and revel in the glories of past victories together!

sections.title: Giving Peer the Seer the bodyguard contract

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I have a weather forecast now please?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I have already told you outerlander; You may have a reading from me when I have a signed contract from a warrior guaranteeing my protection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, I know; I have one right here from Thorvald.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You have not only persuaded one of the Fremennik to act as a servant to me, but you have enlisted the aid of mighty Thorvald himself???

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You may take this forecast with my blessing outerlander. You have offered me the greatest security I can imagine.

sections.title: Talking to Peer the Seer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, about this forecast...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Yes, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I still don't know why you didn't just let me have one anyway in the first place. Surely it means nothing to you?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
