lines.text: That is not true, outerlander. Although I see glimpses of the future all of the time, using my powers brings the attention of the gods to me.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Some of the gods are spiteful and cruel, and I fear if I use my powers too much then I will meet with unpredictable accidents. This is why I needed protection.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay... I... think I understand...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I would like your map of fishing spots.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: I have already told you outerlander; I will not exchange it for anything other than a divination on the weather from our seer himself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What, like this one I have here?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: W-what...? I don't believe it! How did you...? I suppose it doesn't matter, you have my gratitude outerlander!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: With this forecast I will be able to plan a safe course for our next raiding expedition!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Here, outerlander; you may take my map of local fishing patterns with my gratitude!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: If this map of fishing spots is so valuable, why did you give it away to me so easily?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Hmmm? Well, firstly it will be of value to our entire clan, so I have lost nothing from giving it to you.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: The other reason is of course that I have already memorised it, so I can make myself another copy whenever I want!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here. I got you your map.

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Great work outerlander! With this, I can finally catch enough fish to make an honest living from it! Here, have the stupid rare fish.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't see what's so special about this so called rare fish.

lines.speaker: Fisherman
lines.text: Me neither, outerlander. That is why I gave it to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hi there. I got your fish, so can I have that bowstring for Sigli now?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: Ohh... That's a nice fish. Very pleased. Here. Take the bowstring. You fulfilled agreement. only fair I do same. Good work outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So about this bowstring... was it hard to make or something?

lines.speaker: Skulgrimen
lines.text: Not hard. Just a trick to it. Takes skill to learn, but when learnt, easy. Sigli will be happy. Finest bowstring on continent. Will suit his needs perfectly.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Greetings outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here. I have your bowstring. Give me your map to the hunting grounds.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: Well met, outerlander. I see some hunting potential within you. Here, take my map, I was getting too dependent on it for my skill anyway.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you really don't mind giving this away to me?

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: No outerlander... it is hard to explain. That map makes my role as huntsman too easy. I fear my skills are becoming dulled. Now I must track my prey once more.

lines.speaker: Sigli the Huntsman
lines.text: To begin again from scratch... I feel this may keep me sharp.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings again outerlander! How goes your attempts to gain votes with the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I got Sigli's hunting map for you.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Excellent work outerlander! And so quickly, too! Here, you may take my financial report promising reduced sales taxes on all goods.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So cutting sales tax isn't going to ruin your economy here or anything?

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Not at all outerlander; now that we have Sigli's map we can increase the amount of hunts we run, and make up any shortfall that way.

sections.title: Giving the fiscal statement to Yrsa

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. Can I have those boots now? Here is a written statement from Brundt outlining future tax burdens upon Fremennik merchants and shopkeepers for the year.

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Certainly! Let me have a look at what he has written here, just give me a moment...

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Yes, that all appears in order. Tell Olaf to come to me next time for shoes!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hey, these shoes look pretty comfy. Think you could make me a pair like them?

lines.speaker: Yrsa
lines.text: Maybe if you pass your trial and become a full fledged member of the Fremennik...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello Olaf. Do you have a beautiful love song written for me?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: That depends outerlander... Do you have some new boots for me? My feet get so tired roaming the land...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: As a matter of fact - I do!

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Oh! Superb! Those are great! They're just what I was looking for! Here, take this song with my compliments! It is one of my finest works yet!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you think this song is pretty good then?

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Ahhh.... outerlander... it is the most beautiful romantic ballad I have ever been inspired to write...

lines.speaker: Olaf the Bard
lines.text: Only a woman with a heart of icy stone could fail to be moved by its beauty!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks! That sounds perfect!

sections.title: Giving the ballad to the Sailor

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You'll be glad to know I have had a love song written just for you by Olaf. So can I have that flower of yours now?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Oh. It's by Olaf? Hmm. Well, a deal's a deal. I just hope it's better than the usual rubbish he comes up with, or my chances are worse than ever.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: so tell me... who is this woman that you are trying to impress anyway?

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: It's Thora, the longhall barkeep. Please don't tell her though. She's not like the rest of the Fremennik girls, she has a secret desire to see the world.

lines.speaker: Sailor
lines.text: Being a sailor, I can really relate to that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Greetings outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't suppose you have any idea where I could find a map of deep sea fishing spots do you?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Hmmm? Why of course! As the navigator for the Fremennik I keep all of our maps secure right here.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Great! Can I have it?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Have it? Just like that? I think not outerlander. This map shows all of the prime fishing locations nearby.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: It is very valuable to our clan. I am afraid I can not just give it away.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Perhaps I can trade you something for it?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: A trade? For a map of the best fishing spots in a hundred leagues? I will trade it for no less than a weather forecast from our Seer.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: As a navigator, the weather is extremely important for plotting the best course. Unfortunately the Seer is always too busy to help me with a forecast.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where could I get a weather forecast from then?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: I just told you: from the Seer. You will need to persuade him to take the time to make a forecast somehow.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Here's that flower you wanted.

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Incredible! Your merchanting skills might even match my own! I have no choice but to recommend you to the council of elders!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Hello again outerlander! I am amazed once more at your apparent skill at merchanting!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can count on your vote at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Absolutely, outerlander. Your merchanting skills will be a real boon to the Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there!

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Hello outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: That I am outerlander; it is a position that brings my family and I pride.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was wondering if I can count on your vote at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: You wish to become a Fremennik? I may be persuaded to swing my vote to your favour, but you will first need to do a little task for me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How did I know it wouldn't be that simple for your vote?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Calm yourself outerlander. It is but a small task really... I simply require a flower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A flower? What's the catch?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: The catch? Well... it is not just any flower. Someone in this town has an extremely rare flower from a far off land that they picked up on their travels.

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: I would like you to demonstrate your merchanting skills to me, by persuading them to part with it, and then give it to me for my vote.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I guess that doesn't sound too hard...

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Excellent! You will obtain this rare flower for me then?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Okay. I don't think this will be too difficult. Any suggestions on where to start looking for this flower?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: Ah, well outerlander, if I knew where to start looking I would simply do it myself!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No help at ALL?

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: We are a very insular clan, so I would not expect you to have to leave this town to find whatever you need.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You know what? No. This all sounds like a lot of hassle to me, and frankly I just can't be bothered with it right now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll go get someone else to vote for me.

lines.speaker: Sigmund The Merchant
lines.text: As you wish outerlander. If you change your mind, come and see me again; I am very interested in getting my hands on that flower.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what do I have to do to earn your vote at the council again?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: I will not offer my vote to anybody whose bravery in combat I do not trust completely. You must go down that ladder and fight your foe to the death.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, who is my opponent?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Ah, a wise question before entering combat. His name is Koschei the deathless. He is something of a mystery, even to us.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: On one of our regular raiding parties, our longship discovered a man in the frozen waters far north-east of here. We took him aboard our ship, thinking he must be dead.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: To our amazement he was perfectly healthy, even though he must have been in those deadly icy waters for many weeks.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: He has no memory of who he is, or how he came to be there, except for his own name: Koschei. We named him 'The Deathless' because he is seemingly unkillable!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Any combat technique used against him, he learns instantly! He also apparently can heal himself from any wound at will! When he attacks, his weapon moves like a whirlwind!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: He can hide his combat level from his opponents at will as well! He is truly a horribly fierce opponent to face! I am only glad that he has chosen to stay here with us!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: The daylight makes him feel weak, so we have built him his own battleground beneath this building, where he can train his fiercesome skills without being disturbed.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But he can't REALLY be unkillable... ...can he?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Some say he cannot die, for he has hidden his heart outside of his body to be kept forever safe in a duck egg.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Others say he has been cursed by the gods to wander this land forever, never knowing any peace in his life, but only combat.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Some claim that the sword he carries is the source of all his power, and if he should lose it, then exactly one minute later, he will turn back into his true form;

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: A weakened, lame, old man.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what do you believe?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: I believe you shouldn't look a gift horse in the mouth. He is a fearfully powerful warrior, but more importantly; He is on OUR side, not against us.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: He is content testing the battle skills of anyone taking their Fremennik trials of manhood, and I am content knowing that should an enemy ever invade our town,

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: while our warriors are out on a raiding party, Koschei will be able to hold off ANY invader long enough for us to make our return.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I don't really like fighting that much... Isn't there something else I can do to earn your vote at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Yes of course outerlander! If you bring me five raw sardines then I will vote for you instead!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: REALLY?!?!?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: HAHAHAHAHAHA! No, of course not! You are stupid, even by outerlander standards!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: If you want my vote, you must pass my trial. It is as simple as that.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: I was only joking about the raw sardines outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh. Really? Oh. So I have to pass your combat trial?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Yes.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Hello yourself, outerlander. What brings you to dare speak to a mighty Fremennik warrior such as myself?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm... are you a member of the council?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: The Fremennik council of elders? Why, of course I am. I am recognised as one the clans mightiest warriors. What is it to you outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... I was wondering if you could vote for me to become a Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: An outerlander wishes to become a Fremennik!?!? Ha! That is priceless!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Well, let us say that I am not totally against this concept. As a warrior, I appreciate the value of brave and powerful warriors to our clan,

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: and even though you may be an outerlander, I will not hold this against you if you can prove yourself to be fierce of heart in a combat situation to me.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So how can I prove that? You want to fight me? Come on then, bring it on! Right here, right now, buddy!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Hahahahaha! You certainly show some spirit for an outerlander!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: But spirit does not always make a good warrior. It takes both skill and spirit to be so. I have a test that I give all Fremenniks on their path to be a member of the clan.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: My test will challenge both your combat prowess and your bravery equally. Should you pass it you will earn my vote at the council, and more importantly my respect for you as a warrior.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: So what say you, outerlander? Are you prepared for the battle?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Am I prepared? I'll show you what combat's all about, you big sissy barbarian type guy!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Hahahahaha! I'm beginning to like you already, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Then allow me to present you with my challenge; This ladder here will take you to a place of combat. I have placed a special warrior down there to challenge you.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Battle him to the death, and you will pass my challenge. If at any point you wish to leave combat, simply climb back up the ladder, to leave that place.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: If you leave you will of course fail the test. You may retry my test in the future if you fail, but you must stay down there until the death if you wish for my vote at the council.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: You must defeat him three times to prove that you are worthy. The fourth time that you fight him will be to the death, so do not show cowardice.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Is that all? It will be easy!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: No, there is one more important rule;

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: You may not enter the battleground with any armour or weaponry of any kind.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: If you need to place your equipment into your bank account, I recommend that you speak to the seer, who knows a spell that will do that for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'm pretty sure that I can find someone else to vote for me.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Hmm, not so brave after all, outerlander? Perhaps it is for the best. I doubt you have what it takes to pass my challenge.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: You may not enter the battleground with any armour or weaponry of any kind.

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: If you need to place your equipment into your bank account, I recommend that you speak to the seer, who knows a spell that will do that for you.

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: It seems you have some idea of combat after all, outerlander! I will not hold back so much this time!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: Impressive start... But now we fight for real!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: You show some skill at combat... I will hold back no longer! This time you lose your prayer however, and fight like a warrior!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: Prepare to die, [player name]!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: I will crush your dead bones!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: Outerlander... you have made a mistake coming down here!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: What kind of a person calls [him/her]self [player name]?

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: You have no idea of my true power, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: Today is the day you die, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: [Player name]! Meet your doom!

lines.speaker: Koschei the deathless
lines.text: Prepare to face my power, outerlander!

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Hahaha! Well fought outerlander! Now come down from there, you have passed my trial with flying colours!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: But... I don't understand... I did not manage to beat Koschei...

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: I would be honoured to represent you to the council as worthy of being a Fremennik after watching that superb battle!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So can I count on your vote at the council of elders now Thorvald?

lines.speaker: Thorvald the Warrior
lines.text: Absolutely! I watched the entire battle, and was extremely impressed with your bravery in combat!

sections.title: Attempting to climb down the stairs again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'd really rather not go back down there again, now that I have Thorvald's vote at the council of elders.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello there! Thorvald tells me you can deposit my equipment directly into my bank account for me...?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is correct outerlander, but you will need to go and retrieve your equipment from your bank account yourself, manually.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Alas, my spell is not very accurate, so it will deposit everything you have upon you into your bank, not just your armour and weapons.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, please deposit my equipment.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I am sorry outerlander, the spell is not working.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: The task is done. I wish you luck with your test, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks, I'll just go to the bank and sort my inventory out myself.

sections.title: Talking to Peer the Seer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I can bring nothing with me when I enter your house?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is correct outerlander, but as I say, I can use my small skill in magic to send your items directly into your bank account from here.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You will need to manually go to the bank to withdraw them again however. Would you like me to perform this small spell upon you, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I just have to enter by one door of your house, and leave by the other?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is correct outerlander. Be warned it is not as easy as it may at first sound...

sections.title: Interacting with the puzzle pieces and scenery

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Incredible! To have solved my puzzle so quickly! I have no choice but to vote in your favour!

sections.title: Talking to Peer the Seer again

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So you will vote for me at the council?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Absolutely, outerlander. Your wisdom in passing my test marks you as worthy in my eyes.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Hello outerlander. What do you want?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello. I'm looking for members of the council of elders to vote for me to become a Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Are you now? Well that is interesting. Usually outerlanders do not concern themselves with our ways like that.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I am one of the members of the council of elders, and should you be able to prove to me that you have something to offer my clan

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I will vote in your favour at the next meeting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I prove that to you?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Well, I have but a simple test. This building behind me is my house. Inside I have constructed a puzzle.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: As a Seer to the clan, I value intelligence very highly, so you may think of it as an intelligence test of sorts.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: An intelligence test? I thought barbarians were stupid!

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is the opinion that outerlanders usually hold of my people, it is true. But that is because people often confuse knowledge with wisdom.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: My puzzle tests not what you know, but what you can work out. All members of our clan have been tested when they took their trials.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So what exactly does this puzzle consist of, then?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Well, firstly you must enter my house with no items, weapons or armour. Then it is a simple matter of entering through one door and leaving by the other.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I can't take anything in there with me?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: That is correct outerlander. Everything you need to complete the puzzle you will find inside the building. Nothing more.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: So what say you outerlander? You think you have the wit to earn yourself my vote?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, I accept your challenge. I have one small question, however...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Yes, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... you say I can bring nothing with me when I enter your house...

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Yes, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well.....

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Yes, outerlander?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Where is the nearest bank?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: Ah, I see your problem outerlander. The nearest bank to here is the place known to outerlanders as the Seers Village. It is some way South.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I do however have an alternative, should you wish to take it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: And what is that?

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: I can store all the weapons, armour and items that you have upon you directly into your bank account.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: This will tax what little magic I possess however, so you will have to manually travel to the bank to withdraw them again.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: What say you outerlander? you wish me to do this for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes, thank you!

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: The task is done. I wish you luck with your test, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. Nobody touches my stuff but me!

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: As you wish, outerlander.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: You may attempt my little task when you have deposited your equipment in the bank.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thinking about stuff isn't really my 'thing'. I'd rather go kill something. I'll find someone else to vote for me.

lines.speaker: Peer the Seer
lines.text: As you wish, outerlander.

sections.title: Attempting to climb down the ladder or trapdoor before accepting the challenge

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Have you no manners outerlander? This is my home, you will show me the due respect!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Erm.... sorry I guess.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I am trying to become a member of the Fremennik clan! The Chieftain told me that I may be able to gain your vote at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: You wish to stop being an outerlander? I can understand that! I have no reason why I would prevent you becoming a Fremennik...

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: ...but you must first pass a little test for me to prove you are worthy.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of test?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Well, I serve our clan as a navigator. The seas can be a fearful place when you know not where you are heading.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Should something happen to me, all members of our tribe have some basic sense of direction so that they may always return safely home.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: If you are able to demonstrate to me that you too have a good sense of direction then I will recommend you to the rest of the council of elders immediately.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well, how would I go about showing that?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Ah, a simple task! Below this building I have constructed a maze; should you be able to walk from one side to the other that will be proof to me.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: You wish to try my challenge?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: A maze? Is that all? Sure, it sounds simple enough.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: I will warn you outerlander, this maze was designed by myself, and is of the most fiendish complexity!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh really? Watch and learn...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks. I think I can get someone else to vouch for me to the council of elders, without going through a stupid maze like some lab rat.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Well, I am sorry you feel that way outerlander. I cannot vouch for you until you prove to me you have some skill that will benefit our clan somehow.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: What exactly is a 'lab rat' anyway?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Ah, forget it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Man, your maze is pretty tough!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Hahahaha it is the most complex route I have ever devised! I am truly a genius at navigation! The world will remember my name!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can't I do something else for your vote at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: No, you cannot. It is my maze, or nothing.

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Decided you could not solve my labyrinth after all, did you outerlander? Too tricky for you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yeah, it is really confusing down there...

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Hahaha! Don't worry outerlander, you are not the first to fail its puzzle!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Outerlander! You have finished my maze! I am genuinely impressed!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So does that mean I can rely on your vote at the council of elders to allow me into your village?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Of course outerlander! I am nothing if not a man of my word!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Greetings again outerlander! I must say, I am still impressed with the way you moved through my maze!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks. So I can rely on your vote in my favour at the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Swensen the Navigator
lines.text: Absolutely! You will be an asset to the clan!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No way am I doing that maze again!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Greetings again outerlander! How goes your attempts to gain votes with the council of elders?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I only have 1 vote so far.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm... well that is certainly a good start I would say. Keep up the good work!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I only have [2-6] votes so far.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Hmmm... you are doing very well so far, outerlander. Keep up the good work!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Remember: You need to get at least seven council votes to be accepted as a member of the Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: If you need any help with your trials, I suggest you speak to Askeladden. He is currently doing his own trials of manhood to become a true Fremennik.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have seven members of the council prepared to vote in my favour now!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: I know outerlander, for I have been closely monitoring your progress so far!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: Then let us put the formality aside, and let me personally welcome you into the Fremennik! May you bring us honour!

lines.speaker: Brundt the Chieftain
lines.text: From this day onward, you are outerlander no more! In honour of your acceptance into the Fremennik You gain a new name to be known as. You will now be called [Fremennik name].
